field_proposals.rationale: Well-represented NYC borough values with one synonym; normalized to snake_case.
Four distinct implementation status phrases; snake_case normalization.
Two day-range values observed; normalized to snake_case.
Five time-range variants present; normalized to snake_case.
Multi-valued borough list; same normalization as borough. Only four observed.
Only three occurrences, two accepted; one rejected. Insufficient to define a reliable enum.
Contains variable numeric text with no consistent categorical value.
Only two occurrences and one accepted; too thin to proposition as enum or structured field.

key_dispositions.rationale: Internal merged field not relevant for payload.
Already covered by normalized borough enum.
Already covered by normalized boroughs enum.
Single occurrence, likely typo; not meaningful.
Numeric count of bus routes; useful attribute.
Structured date object but very sparse.
Numeric ridership value with adequate occurrence.
Numeric corridor length, well-represented.
Only one occurrence; too sparse to promote.
Extremely sparse directional limit string.
Single occurrence, not generalizable.
Single occurrence.
Single occurrence; likely duplicate of corridor_length_mi.
Sparse directional limit.
Sparse directional limit.
Free-text description of local access rules, present in many submissions.
Single occurrence; not reliable.
Single occurrence.
Single occurrence.
Free-text regulatory summary; useful for corridor description.
Free-text restriction details, present in multiple submissions.
Numeric ridership, though sparse; promote as optional field.
List of bus routes serving corridor; relation to route entities, not corridor identity.
Free-text annotation about routes.
Another list of served routes; relation to routes.

Single occurrence.

proposal.notes: Borough and boroughs enums share the same canonical values except Staten Island not observed in boroughs array; both have escape hats. Several free-text fields provide geometric/policy details. Route-related lists are treated as relation context.
